info.description:
Public REST API for the rattle platform — product configuration, pricing, and quotation.

## Quick Start

1. **Obtain an API key** from the web dashboard under *Settings → API Keys*.
2. **Authenticate** by passing the key as a Bearer token:

```bash
curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer rk_test_REDACTED...' \
     https://www.rattleapp.de/api/v1/products
```

3. **Explore** this spec interactively using the Swagger UI at `/docs/api/interactive`.

## Authentication

Most endpoints require a **Bearer token** (API key) in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer rk_test_REDACTED...
```

API keys are scoped — each key can be granted read/write access to specific resource types:

| Scope | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `products:read` | Read products, areas, groups, options, languages |
| `products:write` | Create/update/delete products and catalog entities |
| `prices:read` | Read price lists and overrides |
| `prices:write` | Create/update/delete price lists |
| `customers:read` | Read customers and contacts |
| `customers:write` | Create/update/delete customers |
| `quotes:read` | Read opportunities and quotes |
| `quotes:write` | Create/update/delete opportunities and quotes |
| `configurations:read` | Read configurations and states |
| `configurations:write` | Calculate and finalize configurations |
| `documents:read` | Read document templates, instances, content blocks, directories, structure, and public links |
| `documents:write` | Create/update/delete templates, content blocks, structure, instances; clone, translate, publish |
| `webhooks:read` | Read webhook subscriptions and deliveries |
| `webhooks:write` | Create/update/delete webhook subscriptions |
| `parts:read` | Read parts, BOM, placements, and baselines |
| `parts:write` | Create/update/delete parts and BOM |
| `connectors:read` | Read connectors, endpoints, tasks, and jobs |
| `connectors:write` | Create/update/delete connectors and run tasks |
| `connectors:execute` | Execute connector tasks and fire webhook triggers |
| `analytics:read` | Read analytics: pipeline, quotes, option selections, part usage |
| `api-keys:manage` | List and view API keys |
| `*` | Wildcard — grants all scopes |

Some administrative endpoints (API key creation, rotation, revocation) require **session authentication** via the web UI.

## Response Format

All responses use a consistent envelope:

- **Single resource:** `{"data": {...}}`
- **Collection:** `{"data": [...], "meta": {"limit": 25, "has_next": true, "next_cursor": "..."}}`

Every resource includes a `links` object with HATEOAS-style `self` and related-resource URLs.

## Errors

Errors follow [RFC 9457 Problem Details](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9457) with `Content-Type: application/problem+json`:

```json
{
  "type": "/problems/not-found",
  "title": "Not Found",
  "status": 404,
  "detail": "The requested resource was not found.",
  "instance": "/api/v1/products/999",
  "request_id": "req_a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8"
}
```

Validation errors (422) include a structured `errors` array:

```json
{
  "type": "/problems/validation-error",
  "title": "Unprocessable Content",
  "status": 422,
  "detail": "The request body contains invalid fields.",
  "errors": [
    {"field": "name", "message": "Field required", "code": "missing"}
  ]
}
```

## Pagination

Collection endpoints use **cursor-based pagination**. Pass `cursor` from `meta.next_cursor` to fetch the next page:

```bash
# First page
GET /api/v1/products?limit=10

# Next page
GET /api/v1/products?limit=10&cursor=eyJhZnRlcl9pZCI6MTB9
```

Default page size is 25 (max 100). When `meta.has_next` is `false`, you've reached the last page.

## Rate Limiting

All responses include rate-limit headers:

| Header | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `X-RateLimit-Limit` | Maximum requests per window |
| `X-RateLimit-Remaining` | Requests remaining in current window |
| `X-RateLimit-Reset` | Unix timestamp when the window resets |
| `Retry-After` | Seconds to wait (only on 429 responses) |

Rate limits vary by subscription plan. Check the `X-RateLimit-Limit` header to see your current allowance.

### Per-Category Rate Limits

Some expensive operations have additional rate limits independent of your plan:

| Category | Limit |
|----------|-------|
| Webhook management | 100/hour |
| Document endpoints | 200/hour |
| Configuration compute | 200/hour |
| API key management | 50/hour |
| Connector write operations | 30/minute |
| Webhook event catalog | 60/minute |
| Translation upserts | 30/minute |
| Constraint checks | 30/minute |
| Price override replace | 30/minute |

## Limits & Quotas

| Resource | Limit |
|----------|-------|
| Max request body size | 1 MB |
| Request timeout | 60 seconds |
| Pagination page size | 25 default, 100 max |
| Batch operations per request | 100 |
| Idempotency key TTL | 24 hours |
| API keys per company | 25 |
| API key max lifetime | 365 days |
| Webhook subscriptions per company | 10 |
| Webhook delivery timeout | 10 seconds |
| Webhook delivery retries | 8 (exponential backoff) |
| Webhook delivery history retention | 30 days |
| BOM computation timeout | 30 seconds |

## Optimistic Concurrency

Bulk-replace endpoints for constraints and price lists use version headers (`X-Constraints-Version`, `X-Price-Lists-Version`) for optimistic locking. Read the current version from a GET response, then include it in your write request to detect concurrent modifications.

## Webhooks

Subscribe to events (e.g., `quote.created`, `product.updated`) and receive HTTPS POST callbacks. Each delivery includes an HMAC-SHA256 signature in the `X-Webhook-Signature` header, computed from the secret you provide at subscription time:

```python
import hmac, hashlib
signature = hmac.new(
    secret.encode(), request.data, hashlib.sha256
).hexdigest()
assert hmac.compare_digest(signature, request.headers['X-Webhook-Signature'])
```

## Multi-Tenancy

All data is scoped to the company associated with your API key. You can never access or modify another company's resources.

## AI Agent Integration (MCP)

This API is also available as an **MCP (Model Context Protocol)** server for AI agents (Claude, GPT, Cursor, etc.). Use the same API key as a Bearer token.

- **Discovery:** `GET /.well-known/mcp.json`
- **Server endpoint:** `/mcp/` (Streamable HTTP)
- **Transport:** `streamable-http`, `sse`

All REST API scopes apply to MCP tools. See the `x-mcp` extension in this spec for machine-readable details.
info.title: rattle REST API

post.description: Create a reusable content block. Optionally include an initial locale with EditorJS block content or a dynamic template_name. A unique key is auto-generated if not provided. Returns 409 if the key already exists for this company.

Create, update, or delete up to 100 content blocks in a single request. Each operation is processed independently — partial failures return 207 Multi-Status with per-operation results. Dynamic blocks cannot be deleted.
Upload an image for use in EditorJS content blocks. Send as multipart/form-data with field name 'image'.

delete.description: Delete a content block and all its locales. Automatically cleans up EditorJS images and decrements storage quota. Dynamic/system blocks cannot be deleted (returns 403).

get.description: Returns the content block metadata along with all associated locale payloads. Each locale includes an `is_stale` flag indicating if the content has changed since the last translation.
get.summary: Get a content block with locales

put.description: Update content block metadata. Dynamic/system blocks only allow `directory_id` and `order_index` changes (returns 403 for other fields). Key changes are validated for uniqueness (409 on conflict).

get.description: List all locale versions for a content block, sorted by language.

post.description: Create a locale for a content block. If a locale with the same language and version already exists, it is updated (upsert). Provide either `blocks` (EditorJS JSON array) or `template_name` (dynamic slot) — not both. Orphaned EditorJS images are automatically cleaned up on update.
post.summary: Create or upsert a content block locale

post.description: Translate the source locale's EditorJS content to the target language via DeepL. Creates or updates the target locale and sets `source_content_hash` for staleness detection. The `is_stale` flag on the target locale will be `true` if the source content changes after translation.
Set the `current_version` pointer for a content block. This determines which locale version is used when resolving content for documents.

get.description: Returns all content directories as a nested hierarchical tree. Root directories have `parent_id: null`; child directories are nested in `children`.

delete.description: Delete a directory. Content blocks in this directory are orphaned to root (directory_id set to null). Child directories are also orphaned to root.

get.description: Return all registered document types and their default layouts, supported output formats, and context requirements.

parameters.description: Opaque cursor for the next page
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: Items per page (server enforces configured maximum)
parameters.name: limit

post.description: Create a document instance from a template for a specific context (quote, configuration, or custom). The instance can then be populated with blocks, attachments, and published to HTML, PDF, or Markdown. Pass `metadata` to store custom data like a title.
Add a new block (chapter / section / container) to a document instance.
Render the instance as HTML, PDF, or Markdown. Optionally create a public link.

Queue an offer PDF render for a customer link + configuration. Returns ``202 Accepted`` with a ``PdfGenerationJob`` handle; poll via ``GET /documents/renders/{job_id}`` and fetch bytes via ``GET /documents/renders/{job_id}/content``.

delete.description: Remove the cached PDF and mark the job expired.

get.description: Stream the completed PDF.  Returns ``202`` if the job is still running, ``410`` if the render has expired or the cache entry was evicted, ``500`` if rendering failed.

202.description: Job still running; retry after a short delay

parameters.description: Opaque cursor for the next page
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: Items per page (server enforces configured maximum)
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Preview which template will win the resolution cascade for a given (doc_type, product_id[, template_id]) tuple. Returns ``source ∈ {explicit, product, company, builtin_default}`` and marks virtual templates with ``is_virtual=true``.

post.description: Assign a template to one or many products. Single product updates in place; multiple products clone the template per product using the specified inheritance mode.

Create a full deep copy of a template including all structure blocks, locale titles, and content block attachments. Content block masters are referenced (not duplicated). Optionally override the name, product, or doc_type.
Mark the template as published so it becomes a candidate for the resolution cascade.

get.description: Return the fully resolved template tree (structure blocks, content blocks, and locales) with visibility conditions filtered against an optional configuration.

Returns the full hierarchical tree of structure blocks (chapters, sections, containers). Pass `?language=DE` to resolve locale-specific titles. Extend-mode variants are automatically synced with their origin template before returning.

post.description: Add a chapter, section, container, repeater, or placeholder block to the template's structure tree. Slugs must be unique per template (auto-generated if omitted). Returns 403 for link-mode templates (read-only structure).

delete.description: Delete a structure block and all its descendants (children, locales, attachments). Returns 403 for link-mode templates.

post.description: Attach a reusable content block to a structure block. The content block's locale will be resolved at render time based on the document's language. Supports conditions for conditional inclusion.

put.description: Set the translated title for a structure block in a specific language. Creates the locale if it doesn't exist, updates it if it does.

post.description: Translate all structure block titles and attached content block locales to the target language via DeepL. Creates or updates locale rows for each block. Sets `source_content_hash` on content locales for staleness tracking. Returns counts of translated titles and content locales.

get.summary: Validate a configuration against a template

post.description: Create a derived template linked to this template as its origin. **link**: read-only mirror (structure writes return 403). **extend**: inherits origin structure, auto-syncs on read, allows additions. **fork**: deep copy with origin reference (independent after creation).

get.description: Returns a paginated list of groups. Use `search` to filter by name, `area_id` to filter by area assignment.

parameters.description: Opaque cursor for the next page
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: Items per page (server enforces configured maximum)
parameters.name: limit

post.description: Execute up to 100 create/update/delete/upsert operations on groups. Returns 200 if all succeed, 207 if any fail.

get.description: Returns all areas the group is currently linked to. Not paginated.
get.summary: List areas linked to a group

post.description: Link a group to one or more areas. Already-linked areas are silently skipped (idempotent).

delete.description: Remove the group-area association and clean up area-specific data (OptionAreaConfig, OptionPriceOverride, OptionAdvancedPrice rows).
delete.summary: Unlink a group from an area

post.description: Deep-copy the group including all options.

get.description: Returns options belonging to this group. Not paginated.

post.description: Fire a connector task with optional runtime context.
Create or update a customer from an external system.
Create or update multiple customers in one request.
Submit a generic event for processing.
Create an opportunity with optional configuration and quote from an external system.
Create or update part placements for a product/area.

parameters.description: Webhook path suffix configured on the trigger
parameters.name: suffix

post.description: Fire a webhook-type trigger by its configured path suffix. External systems POST to this endpoint to trigger a connector task.
post.summary: Fire a webhook trigger by path

get.description: Returns the effective price considering overrides.

get.description: List condition-based price overrides for an option. Not paginated.

Transitions to Released state. Obsoletes the previous released revision for the same part.

Execute up to 100 create/update/delete/upsert operations on products. Returns 200 if all succeed, 207 if any fail.

get.description: Retrieve a single product by ID. Use `expand` to inline related resources. Supported expansions: `areas`, `areas.groups`, `areas.groups.options`, `gallery`.
List all areas assigned to this product with their assignment metadata (area_id, area_name, order_index, enabled). Not paginated.

post.description: Create a subscription for one or more event types. If you provide a `secret`, each delivery will include an `X-Webhook-Signature` header containing the HMAC-SHA256 hex digest of the request body, signed with your secret. The secret is returned only in the creation response — store it securely. The URL must use HTTPS and cannot point to private/internal networks.

get.description: Returns the catalog of all event types available for webhook subscriptions.

parameters.name: cursor

post.description: Generate a new signing secret. The new secret is returned in the response (shown only once). The old secret remains valid briefly during rollover — verify signatures against both secrets during the transition.
Queue a test delivery to verify the webhook endpoint.

tags.description: Products are the top-level entities in your catalog. Each product contains areas, groups, and options that define its configurable structure. Scope: `products:read`, `products:write`.
tags.name: Products

tags.description: Areas represent sections or modules within a product configuration (e.g., 'Exterior', 'Interior'). Each area contains option groups. Scope: `products:read`, `products:write`.
tags.name: Areas

tags.description: Upload, replace, and delete images for products, areas, and options. Supports primary images, background images, galleries (up to 10), and area-specific option overrides. All uploads accept `multipart/form-data`. Scope: `products:read`, `products:write`.
tags.name: Images

tags.description: Option groups are containers for related configuration choices within an area (e.g., 'Color', 'Material'). Groups can be duplicated with all their options. Scope: `products:read`, `products:write`.
tags.name: Groups

tags.description: Options are the individual selectable choices within a group (e.g., 'Red', 'Blue'). Each option has a base price that can be overridden per price-list/area combination. Scope: `products:read`, `products:write`, `prices:read`.
tags.name: Options

tags.description: Price lists represent different pricing contexts (e.g., currencies, regions, customer tiers). Each price list can override option prices. Scope: `prices:read`, `prices:write`.
tags.name: Price Lists

tags.description: Customer records with optional contact persons. Customers are linked to opportunities and quotes. Scope: `customers:read`, `customers:write`.
tags.name: Customers

tags.description: Supported languages for product translations. One language is marked as the base language. Scope: `products:read`, `products:write`.
tags.name: Languages

tags.description: Rules that forbid certain option or area combinations. Includes pair-level forbidden combinations and JSON-based rules for complex logic. Uses optimistic concurrency via `X-Constraints-Version`. Scope: `products:read`, `products:write`.
tags.name: Constraints

tags.description: Technical attributes (number, range, boolean, text) that can be attached to products, areas, or options for specification sheets and filtering. Scope: `products:read`, `products:write`.
tags.name: Attributes

tags.description: The configuration engine resolves option selections against constraints and computes pricing. Configuration states are identified by hash for deduplication. Scope: `configurations:read`, `configurations:write`.
tags.name: Configurations

tags.description: Sales opportunities track deals through stages (qualification → drafting → presented → negotiation → won/lost). Each opportunity has one or more quotes. Scope: `quotes:read`, `quotes:write`.
tags.name: Opportunities

tags.description: Quotes contain line items with pricing. They go through status transitions (draft → approved → presented → accepted/rejected) and support revisions for version control. Scope: `quotes:read`, `quotes:write`.
tags.name: Quotes

tags.description: Full CMS/CCMS document workspace. **Content Blocks** are reusable EditorJS rich-text components organized in directories, with per-language locales and version tracking. **Structure** defines the hierarchical chapter/section tree of a template, with locale titles and content block attachments. **Templates** support deep cloning, inheritance variants (link/extend/fork), and full-template translation via DeepL. **Instances** are rendered snapshots of a template for a specific context (quote, configuration), with publishable output (HTML/PDF/Markdown) and shareable public links. Scope: `documents:read`, `documents:write`.
tags.name: Documents

tags.description: Subscribe to real-time event notifications via HTTPS callbacks. Deliveries include HMAC-SHA256 signatures for verification. Failed deliveries are retried with exponential backoff. Scope: `webhooks:read`, `webhooks:write`.
tags.name: Webhooks

tags.description: Manage API keys for programmatic access. The plaintext key is shown only once at creation — store it securely. Requires session authentication for create/rotate/revoke operations. Scope: `api-keys:manage`.
tags.name: API Keys

tags.description: External system integrations (ERP, CRM, PIM). Connectors define endpoints and tasks that can be triggered manually or on schedule. Jobs track execution history. Scope: `connectors:read`, `connectors:write`.
tags.name: Connectors

tags.description: PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) parts catalog. Parts have placements on areas and Bill of Materials (BOM) hierarchies for manufacturing. Scope: `parts:read`, `parts:write`.
tags.name: Parts

tags.description: Frozen snapshots of a product's part structure at a point in time. Used for engineering change management and audit trails. Scope: `parts:read`, `parts:write`.
tags.name: Baselines

tags.description: Read and update company-level settings (name, default language, URL prefix). Scope: `products:read`, `products:write`.
tags.name: Company

tags.description: Batch/bulk operations for creating, updating, deleting, or upserting multiple entities in a single request. Supports per-item error handling (partial success). Max 100 operations per request. Scope: varies by resource.
tags.name: Batch

tags.description: Revision tracking for parts with lifecycle states (Draft → Review → Released → Obsolete). Only Draft revisions can be edited or deleted. Releasing a revision locks it and obsoletes the previous released revision. Scope: `parts:read`, `parts:write`.
tags.name: Item Revisions

tags.description: Technical document metadata (CAD files, drawings, specifications) associated with parts. Documents can be linked to parts or specific revisions. Scope: `parts:read`, `parts:write`.
tags.name: Part Documents

tags.description: Per-option price overrides scoped to area and price list combinations. Supports individual CRUD and bulk replace operations. Scope: `prices:read`, `prices:write`.
tags.name: Price Overrides

tags.description: Manage catalog filter dimensions and values for the public product selection page. Dimensions group filterable attributes (e.g. Product Line, Material); values are the selectable options within each dimension. Scope: `products:read`, `products:write`.
tags.name: Catalog Filters

tags.description: Receive data from external systems (CRM, ERP). Supports customer upsert, opportunity creation, part import, and connector triggering. Scope: `webhooks:inbound`, `customers:write`, `quotes:write`, `connectors:execute`.
tags.name: Inbound Webhooks

tags.description: Named groups that organize areas within a product. Scope: `products:read`, `products:write`.
tags.name: Area Groups

tags.description: Pre-authenticated configurator links for specific products. Scope: `customers:read`, `customers:write`.
tags.name: Customer Links

tags.description: Product catalog translations and dictionary entries. Scope: `products:read`, `products:write`.
tags.name: Translations

tags.description: Bulk export endpoints streaming NDJSON for incremental sync. Scope: varies by resource.
tags.name: Export

tags.description: Read-only analytics: pipeline snapshots, quote analytics, option selection facts, and part usage facts. Scope: `analytics:read`.
tags.name: Analytics

tags.description: Version control branches for product configuration. Scope: `parts:read`, `parts:write`.
tags.name: Branches

tags.description: Branch review workflows — create, review, approve, and merge pull requests. Scope: `parts:read`, `parts:write`.
tags.name: Pull Requests

tags.description: Engineering Change Requests (ECR) for tracking proposed changes to product configurations. Scope: `parts:read`, `parts:write`.
tags.name: Change Requests

tags.description: Engineering Change Orders (ECO) with impacts and approvals. Track item-level changes and approval workflows. Scope: `parts:read`, `parts:write`.
tags.name: Change Orders

tags.description: Video links and 3D model links for products. Scope: `products:read`, `products:write`.
tags.name: Product Media
